---
title: Lesson Path
description: Recommended path through the FreeCampus Python course.
---

## How to use this course

If you are new to programming, follow the lessons in order. If you already know
some Python, use the checkpoints and debugging exercises to decide where to
start.

::: {.key-idea}
Programming is not memorizing every command. Programming is learning how to
break a problem into small steps, run experiments, read feedback, and improve.
:::

## The path

::: {.course-grid}

::: {.course-card}
### 1. First contact

- [Start Here](00-start-here.qmd)
- [Colab Quickstart](01-colab-quickstart.qmd)
- [Install Python, VS Code, and Jupyter](02-install-python-vscode-jupyter.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 2. Core Python

- [Values, Variables, and Types](03-values-variables-types.qmd)
- [Strings, Input, and Output](04-strings-input-output.qmd)
- [Conditionals](05-conditionals.qmd)
- [Loops](06-loops.qmd)
- [Lists and Tuples](07-lists-tuples.qmd)
- [Dictionaries and Sets](08-dictionaries-sets.qmd)
- [Functions](09-functions.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 3. Independent Python

- [Debugging and Error Messages](10-debugging-errors.qmd)
- [Files and Exceptions](11-files-exceptions.qmd)
- [Modules, Packages, and Environments](12-modules-packages-environments.qmd)
- [Testing, Style, and Linting](13-testing-style-linting.qmd)
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 4. Deeper and applied Python

- [Classes and Object-Oriented Programming](14-classes-oop.qmd)
- [Python Power Tools](15-python-power-tools.qmd)
- [Scientific Python](16-scientific-python.qmd)
- [Data Science](17-data-science.qmd)
- [AI Libraries](18-ai-libraries.qmd)
- [Capstone](19-capstone.qmd)
:::

:::

## Study routine for every lesson

1. Read the analogy first.
2. Predict what each short code example will do.
3. Run the code in Colab, Jupyter, or VS Code.
4. Explain the result in your own words.
5. Change one small thing and run again.
6. Try the quiz before opening the solution.
7. Fix the debugging exercise.

## Colab practice

Open a blank notebook at <https://colab.new>. When repository notebooks are
added, lessons can link directly to them using the standard Colab badge.
